title: Fitter and Turner
description: A Fitter and Turner in India works on assembling, installing, and repairing machinery, tools, and other equipment. They use hand tools, power tools, and precision instruments to perform tasks such as fitting, turning, and drilling. Their work involves attention to detail, manual dexterity, and problem-solving skills to ensure that equipment is functioning correctly and efficiently.
top_govt_colleges: National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT) affiliated colleges
top_private_colleges: Maharashtra State Board of Technical Education (MSBTE) affiliated colleges
Delhi State Industrial and Technical Training (DSIT) affiliated colleges

career_progression.year_0_2: Apprenticeship or entry-level positions in manufacturing, maintenance, or repair shops
career_progression.year_3_5: Promotions to supervisory or team lead roles, or specialization in a specific area like CNC machining or robotics
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior roles in quality control, production planning, or training and development
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting a small business offering repair or maintenance services, or creating a product line using skills learned as a Fitter and Turner
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities in countries like the UAE, Saudi Arabia, or Singapore, where there is a high demand for skilled tradespeople

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Initial years may involve working long hours, including night shifts, and facing physical demands of the job
common_misconceptions: Fitter and Turners are only skilled in manual labor
The job is limited to manufacturing industries
